message:
fix(ft-api): tensor_logit composes through clamp+sub+div+log for autograd [bd-1tax]

tensor_logit was severed-autograd: it extracted tensor_values via
self.tensor_tape.tensor(...).storage()? + meta.clone(), computed the
logit element-wise in plain f64, and rebuilt a fresh
requires_grad=false leaf — silently dropping the gradient that
should reach the input. PyTorch's torch.special.logit is fully
differentiable.

Composition:
  logit(x, eps?) = log(clamp(x, eps, 1 - eps) / (1 - clamp(x, ...)))

Implemented through tensor_clamp (when eps is provided) +
tensor_sub (against a broadcast ones tensor) + tensor_div +
tensor_log — all autograd-aware. The chain rule delivers the
analytical derivative d/dx logit(x) = 1 / (x * (1 - x)) automatically
on the unclamped path; clamped values pass through tensor_clamp's
existing zero-grad-at-saturated-positions backward.

Test: logit_propagates_gradients_through_input asserts
d/dx logit(x) = 1/(x*(1-x)) element-wise for input [0.25, 0.5, 0.75]
under sum loss. Pre-fix, tensor_gradient(input) returned None.
Existing forward tests (logit_inverse_of_sigmoid,
logit_with_eps_clamping) continue to pass — bit-for-bit forward
parity preserved.

Files closure for one slice of frankentorch-1tax (the umbrella
"statistical/special ops with severed-autograd" tracking bead);
remaining children (cov, corrcoef, mode, quantile, erfinv, digamma,
polygamma, multigammaln, entr, xlogy, xlog1py, logcumsumexp) follow
the same pattern.

Workspace: 1122 ft-api tests green (1121 + 1 new), clippy clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -8,6 +8,7 @@
 {"id":"frankentorch-1le","title":"Investigate persistent beads SQLite integrity warning after clean rebuild","description":"## Context
After a full non-destructive rebuild of `.beads/beads.db` from canonical `issues.jsonl`, `br doctor --json` still reports:

`sqlite.integrity_check: database disk image is malformed: page 17 is never used`

This persists even on the freshly rebuilt DB, while all operational checks are otherwise healthy (`br ready --json` is empty, sync metadata matches, blocked cache is not stale).

## Goal
Determine whether this warning reflects:
1. a genuine low-level SQLite file/layout problem,
2. expected frankensqlite behavior that `br doctor` should not flag as suspicious, or
3. a beads/br bug in the integrity-check path.

## Acceptance
- Reproduce the warning on the current canonical DB
- Compare against a freshly rebuilt temp DB
- Gather enough evidence to classify the issue as workspace-local vs tool-level
- If it is tool-level or not safely fixable from this repo, document the finding clearly and close or hand off with evidence","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-03T13:56:40.262095265Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T14:00:06.096361407Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T14:00:06.096186135Z","close_reason":"Reproduced the integrity warning on canonical and rebuilt DBs, proved a plain SQLite VACUUM clears it, then observed br doctor --repair reintroduce the warning on a different page number while keeping logical metadata healthy; this classifies the issue as a tool-level beads/frankensqlite write-path problem rather than repo-local issue-data corruption","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-1nn","title":"Enforce index_add/index_copy shape + rank rules","description":"Audit: tensor_index_add/index_copy accept mismatched src/index shapes and non-1D indices; they silently ignore extra indices. Fix by enforcing index rank=1 and src shape matches input except dim, with src_dim == index length. Add tests for mismatch errors and f32 parity.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T12:57:29.258566505Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T13:01:14.230978644Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T13:01:14.230629796Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-1s2","title":"Add F.pad reflect/replicate/circular modes","description":"Extend tensor_pad to support reflect, replicate, circular padding modes. Currently only constant padding. Essential for vision preprocessing.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-02T19:49:10.441649598Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T19:53:41.804652781Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T19:53:41.804379905Z","close_reason":"Implemented tensor_pad_mode with reflect, replicate, circular modes. 6 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"frankentorch-1tax","title":"Statistical / special ops with severed-autograd: cov, corrcoef, mode, quantile, logit, erfinv, digamma, polygamma, multigammaln, entr, xlogy, xlog1py, logcumsumexp","description":"These ft-api ops all extract tensor values via self.tensor_values + return self.tensor_tape.leaf(... false), silently severing the autograd tape on tracked inputs (the same pattern recently fixed for SELU / CELU / Threshold / Hardtanh / Unfold / Fold / index_* / scatter_* / masked_scatter / PReLU + 6 shrink-family activations). Some have explicit fail-loud guards (logit doesn't, erfinv does, digamma doesn't, etc.) but the contract is inconsistent. Pick each off and either compose through autograd primitives (preferred where the math admits it) or add a uniform fail-loud guard. PyTorch's torch.cov / torch.corrcoef / torch.special.{logit,digamma,polygamma,...} are all differentiable. Children: each op gets its own fix bead.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-29T20:24:23.438424203Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-29T20:24:23.438424203Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-2er","title":"Add custom autograd Function API (torch.autograd.Function equivalent)","description":"Users cannot currently define custom backward functions. This is arguably the single most impactful missing feature for real-world use — without it, any layer whose backward isn't built into FrankenTorch's op set is impossible to implement.

PyTorch's torch.autograd.Function API allows users to:
1. Define a custom forward() that registers itself on the autograd tape
2. Define a custom backward() that computes gradients for the custom op
3. Save tensors for backward via ctx.save_for_backward()

Implementation plan:
1. Add a CustomFunction trait in ft-autograd with forward/backward methods
2. Add a TensorNodeOp::Custom variant to the tensor tape
3. Add session.apply_custom_function() to ft-api that invokes the user's forward, registers the op on the tape, and stores the backward closure
4. During backward traversal, call the user's backward function when encountering Custom nodes
5. Handle save_for_backward by capturing TensorNodeIds in the closure context

Key design decisions:
- The backward closure must receive upstream gradient and return downstream gradients
- Must respect no_grad context (custom forward inside no_grad should not record on tape)
- Must work with the existing deterministic backward scheduler
- Error handling: user backward functions should return Result, not panic

Tests:
- Unit test: custom ReLU equivalent (forward: max(0,x), backward: 1 if x>0 else 0)
- Unit test: custom function with multiple inputs/outputs
- Unit test: custom function inside no_grad context (should not record)
- Unit test: custom function backward produces correct gradients (numerical gradient check)
- E2E test: train a model with a custom layer and verify loss decreases
- Edge case: nested custom functions
- Edge case: custom function that saves tensors for backward","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-07T04:16:46.006446323Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-07T20:15:34.162735830Z","closed_at":"2026-04-07T20:15:34.162441333Z","close_reason":"Core functionality already exists: tensor_apply_function() with FunctionCtx (save_for_backward, needs_input_grad) + backward closure. Extensive tests in ft-autograd (lines 17484+) and used internally by ft-api for ~12 ops. The closure-based API is the Rust-idiomatic equivalent of PyTorch's class-based torch.autograd.Function. A trait wrapper could be added later for API sugar but is not blocking.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-2os","title":"Implement slogdet, pinverse, kthvalue, linalg_cross, linalg_vector_norm","description":"Add numerically stable linalg and selection ops: slogdet (sign+log determinant), pinverse (SVD-based pseudoinverse), kthvalue (k-th smallest), linalg_cross (3D cross product), linalg_vector_norm (flexible vector norm). Used in scientific computing, regularization, and model analysis.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-28T14:15:54.437563761Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-29T02:09:46.045687331Z","closed_at":"2026-03-29T02:09:46.045431557Z","close_reason":"slogdet/cross already existed. Implemented pinverse (SVD-based), kthvalue (with index), linalg_vector_norm (L0/L1/L2/Linf). 9 tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"frankentorch-2rl","title":"Wire f32 tensor comparisons and float classify to dispatch","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T04:57:24.571301561Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T05:01:04.720640114Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T05:01:04.720320760Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
